---
title: "simulation"
format: html
editor: visual
---

# Simulation:

```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(lme4)
library(MASS) 

```

# Step 1: Specify data-generating function

important: cosine similarity and agreement likely to be correlated, so I add rho = 0.3 into sim between the two variables.

```{r}

simulate_agreement_and_cosine_data_shared_RE <- function(
  n_papers = 50,
  n_dims   = 9,

  # fixed effects...
  # ...for RQ1
  beta_intercept_agreement  = qlogis(0.78), # value in qlogis is human-human interrater agreement
  regcheckhuman_agreement = qlogis(0.75), # value in qlogis is mean regcheck-human agreement
  
  # ...for RQ2
  beta_intercept_cosine  = 0.80, # baseline mean human-human cosine similarity
  regcheckhuman_cosine = 0.74, # baseline mean regcheck-human cosine similarity
  

  # random intercepts for models 
  sd_dimension_agreement = .7, # random effect for dimension on agreement; logit scale
  sd_paper_agreement     = .7, # random effect for paper on agreement; logit scale
  sd_rater_agreement = 0.2, # random effect for raters (both slots) on agreement; logit scale
  sd_human_pair_agreement = 0.2, # random effect for human_pair on agreement; logit scale
  sd_dimension_cosine   = 0.06, # baseline random effect for dimension on cosine
  sd_paper_cosine       = 0.06, # baseline random effect for paper on cosine
  sd_rater_cosine = 0.04, # random effect for raters (both slots) on cosine
  sd_human_pair_cosine = 0.04, # random effect for human_pair on cosine

  # intercorrelations
  rho_row = 0.3, # correlation between agreement and cosine similarity
  sd_row_agreement = 0.7,   # defined on logit scale
  sd_row_cosine   = 0.09,  # baseline row-level cosine SD

  # design
  number_of_raters = 5, # total number of human raters in the pool
  raters_per_paper = 2, # preregistered design: 2 coders per paper
  seed = NULL, 
  clip_cosine_01 = TRUE # clipping can attenuate mean differences when values are near 0/1
) {
  
  # define mean differences against the human-human reference
  beta_regcheck_agreement <- regcheckhuman_agreement - beta_intercept_agreement
  beta_regcheck_cosine <- regcheckhuman_cosine - beta_intercept_cosine
  
  # set seed if specified
  if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)
  # stop if correlation values are impossible
  stopifnot(abs(rho_row) < 1)
  stopifnot(number_of_raters >= 2)
  stopifnot(raters_per_paper == 2)
  stopifnot(raters_per_paper <= number_of_raters)
  stopifnot(sd_rater_agreement >= 0)
  stopifnot(sd_human_pair_agreement >= 0)
  stopifnot(sd_rater_cosine >= 0)
  stopifnot(sd_human_pair_cosine >= 0)

  # arbitrary identifiers for papers and dimensions
  paper_ids <- paste0("P", seq_len(n_papers))
  dims      <- paste0("D", seq_len(n_dims))
  rater_ids <- seq_len(number_of_raters)
  rater_pool_labels <- paste0("R", rater_ids)

  # Randomly assign two human coders per paper from the full rater pool.
  assigned_raters <- lapply(
    seq_len(n_papers),
    function(i) sort(sample(rater_ids, raters_per_paper, replace = FALSE))
  )
  names(assigned_raters) <- paper_ids

  # Build preregistered comparison rows:
  # human1_human2, regcheck_human1, regcheck_human2
  data <- bind_rows(lapply(paper_ids, function(pid) {
    h1 <- assigned_raters[[pid]][1]
    h2 <- assigned_raters[[pid]][2]
    pair_id <- paste0("R", h1, "__R", h2)
    h1_label <- paste0("R", h1)
    h2_label <- paste0("R", h2)

    bind_rows(
      tibble(
        paper_id = pid,
        dimension = dims,
        comparison_type = "human1_human2",
        rater1_id = h1_label,
        rater2_id = h2_label,
        human_pair = pair_id
      ),
      tibble(
        paper_id = pid,
        dimension = dims,
        comparison_type = "regcheck_human1",
        rater1_id = "RegCheck",
        rater2_id = h1_label,
        human_pair = pair_id
      ),
      tibble(
        paper_id = pid,
        dimension = dims,
        comparison_type = "regcheck_human2",
        rater1_id = "RegCheck",
        rater2_id = h2_label,
        human_pair = pair_id
      )
    )
  }))

  # ensure factors are correctly specified 
  data$comparison_type <- factor(
    data$comparison_type,
    levels = c("human1_human2", "regcheck_human1", "regcheck_human2")
  )
  data$dimension <- factor(data$dimension)
  data$paper_id  <- factor(data$paper_id)
  rater_levels <- c("RegCheck", rater_pool_labels)
  data$rater1_id <- factor(data$rater1_id, levels = rater_levels)
  data$rater2_id <- factor(data$rater2_id, levels = rater_levels)
  data$human_pair <- factor(data$human_pair)
  human_pair_levels <- levels(data$human_pair)

  # Shared random effect draws (raw ~ N(0,1)), then scaled per outcome
  
  # raw draws
  u_dim_raw <- rnorm(n_dims, 0, 1)
  names(u_dim_raw) <- dims
  
  v_paper_raw <- rnorm(n_papers, 0, 1)
  names(v_paper_raw) <- paper_ids
  rater_raw <- rnorm(length(rater_levels), 0, 1)
  names(rater_raw) <- rater_levels
  human_pair_raw <- rnorm(length(human_pair_levels), 0, 1)
  names(human_pair_raw) <- human_pair_levels

  # scale for agreement
  u_dim_agreement <- sd_dimension_agreement * u_dim_raw
  v_paper_agreement <- sd_paper_agreement * v_paper_raw
  rater_agreement <- sd_rater_agreement * rater_raw
  human_pair_agreement <- sd_human_pair_agreement * human_pair_raw

  # scale for cosine
  u_dim_cosine <- sd_dimension_cosine * u_dim_raw
  v_paper_cosine <- sd_paper_cosine * v_paper_raw
  rater_cosine <- sd_rater_cosine * rater_raw
  human_pair_cosine <- sd_human_pair_cosine * human_pair_raw
  

  # Linear predictors (without row-level noise)
  lp_agree <- beta_intercept_agreement +
    beta_regcheck_agreement * (data$comparison_type != "human1_human2") +
    u_dim_agreement[as.character(data$dimension)] +
    v_paper_agreement[as.character(data$paper_id)] +
    rater_agreement[as.character(data$rater1_id)] +
    rater_agreement[as.character(data$rater2_id)] +
    human_pair_agreement[as.character(data$human_pair)]

  mu_cos <- beta_intercept_cosine +
    beta_regcheck_cosine * (data$comparison_type != "human1_human2") +
    u_dim_cosine[as.character(data$dimension)] +
    v_paper_cosine[as.character(data$paper_id)] +
    rater_cosine[as.character(data$rater1_id)] +
    rater_cosine[as.character(data$rater2_id)] +
    human_pair_cosine[as.character(data$human_pair)]

  # add row-level noise from correlations
  Sigma <- matrix(
    c(sd_row_agreement^2, rho_row * sd_row_agreement * sd_row_cosine,
      rho_row * sd_row_agreement * sd_row_cosine, sd_row_cosine^2),
    nrow = 2, byrow = TRUE
  )
  
  eps <- MASS::mvrnorm(n = nrow(data), mu = c(0, 0), Sigma = Sigma)
  eps_agree <- eps[, 1]  # added on logit scale
  eps_cos   <- eps[, 2]  # added on cosine scale

  # Outcomes
  data$agreement <- rbinom(nrow(data), 1, plogis(lp_agree + eps_agree))
  data$agree <- data$agreement
  data$cos_sim   <- mu_cos + eps_cos
  if (clip_cosine_01) data$cos_sim <- pmin(1, pmax(0, data$cos_sim))

  data
}

```

# Step 2: define helpers for simulation

```{r}

# Define two analyses: one for comparing agreement, one for comparing cosine
analyze_simulated_data <- function(data, 
                                   conf = 0.95, 
                                   n_draws_delta = 4000) {
  
  out <- list()
  z <- qnorm(1 - (1 - conf) / 2)
  a <- (1 - conf) / 2

  classify_claim <- function(ci_lo, ci_hi, margin) {
    if (is.na(ci_lo) || is.na(ci_hi) || is.na(margin)) return("no_fit")
    if (ci_lo > -margin) return("noninferior")
    if (ci_hi < -margin) return("inferior")
    "inconclusive"
  }

  hh_cos <- data$cos_sim[data$comparison_type == "human1_human2"]
  hh_agreement <- data$agreement[data$comparison_type == "human1_human2"]
  out$rq1_margin <- 0.5 * stats::sd(hh_agreement, na.rm = TRUE)
  out$rq2_margin <- 0.5 * stats::sd(hh_cos, na.rm = TRUE)

  # RQ1 model (agreement)
  fit_rq1 <- suppressWarnings(tryCatch(
    glmer(
      agree ~ comparison_type + (1 | paper_id) + (1 | dimension) + (1 | rater1_id) + (1 | rater2_id),
      data = data,
      family = binomial(link = "logit"),
      control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa", optCtrl = list(maxfun = 2e5))
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  ))

  # RQ2 model (cosine)
  fit_rq2 <- suppressWarnings(tryCatch(
    lmer(
      cos_sim ~ comparison_type + (1 | paper_id) + (1 | dimension) + (1 | rater1_id) + (1 | rater2_id),
      data = data,
      control = lmerControl(check.conv.singular = "ignore")
    ),
    error = function(e) NULL
  ))

  out$ok_rq1 <- !is.null(fit_rq1)
  out$ok_rq2 <- !is.null(fit_rq2)

  # RQ1: contrast on probability scale via MVN draws of fixed effects
  out$rq1_delta_hat <- NA_real_
  out$rq1_ci_lo <- NA_real_
  out$rq1_ci_hi <- NA_real_
  if (out$ok_rq1) {
    fe <- fixef(fit_rq1)
    V <- tryCatch(vcov(fit_rq1), error = function(e) NULL)
    needed <- c("(Intercept)", "comparison_typeregcheck_human1", "comparison_typeregcheck_human2")

    if (!is.null(V) && all(needed %in% names(fe)) && all(needed %in% rownames(V))) {
      alpha_hat <- unname(fe["(Intercept)"])
      beta1_hat <- unname(fe["comparison_typeregcheck_human1"])
      beta2_hat <- unname(fe["comparison_typeregcheck_human2"])

      out$rq1_delta_hat <- 0.5 * (plogis(alpha_hat + beta1_hat) + plogis(alpha_hat + beta2_hat)) - plogis(alpha_hat)

      V3 <- V[needed, needed, drop = FALSE]
      draws <- MASS::mvrnorm(
        n = n_draws_delta,
        mu = c(alpha_hat, beta1_hat, beta2_hat),
        Sigma = V3
      )

      delta_prob <- 0.5 * (plogis(draws[, 1] + draws[, 2]) + plogis(draws[, 1] + draws[, 3])) - plogis(draws[, 1])
      ci <- unname(quantile(delta_prob, probs = c(a, 1 - a), na.rm = TRUE))
      out$rq1_ci_lo <- ci[1]
      out$rq1_ci_hi <- ci[2]
    }
  }
  out$rq1_claim <- classify_claim(out$rq1_ci_lo, out$rq1_ci_hi, out$rq1_margin)
  out$rq1_claim_noninferior <- as.integer(out$rq1_claim == "noninferior")
  out$rq1_claim_inferior <- as.integer(out$rq1_claim == "inferior")

  # RQ2: contrast on cosine scale
  out$rq2_delta_hat <- NA_real_
  out$rq2_ci_lo <- NA_real_
  out$rq2_ci_hi <- NA_real_
  if (out$ok_rq2) {
    fe <- fixef(fit_rq2)
    V <- tryCatch(vcov(fit_rq2), error = function(e) NULL)
    needed <- c("comparison_typeregcheck_human1", "comparison_typeregcheck_human2")

    if (!is.null(V) && all(needed %in% names(fe)) && all(needed %in% rownames(V))) {
      beta1 <- unname(fe["comparison_typeregcheck_human1"])
      beta2 <- unname(fe["comparison_typeregcheck_human2"])
      out$rq2_delta_hat <- 0.5 * (beta1 + beta2)

      V2 <- V[needed, needed, drop = FALSE]
      w <- matrix(c(0.5, 0.5), nrow = 1)
      se_delta <- sqrt(as.numeric(w %*% V2 %*% t(w)))
      out$rq2_ci_lo <- out$rq2_delta_hat - z * se_delta
      out$rq2_ci_hi <- out$rq2_delta_hat + z * se_delta
    }
  }
  out$rq2_claim <- classify_claim(out$rq2_ci_lo, out$rq2_ci_hi, out$rq2_margin)
  out$rq2_claim_noninferior <- as.integer(out$rq2_claim == "noninferior")
  out$rq2_claim_inferior <- as.integer(out$rq2_claim == "inferior")

  as.data.frame(out)
}




```

# Step 3: Build parameter grid

```{r}

# define helper function for building full parameter grid
make_param_grid <- function(
  beta_intercept_agreement,
  regcheckhuman_agreement,
  n_papers,
  n_dims = 9,
  number_of_raters,
  raters_per_paper,
  beta_intercept_cosine,
  regcheckhuman_cosine,
  sd_dimension_agreement,
  sd_paper_agreement,
  sd_rater_agreement,
  sd_human_pair_agreement,
  sd_dimension_cosine,
  sd_paper_cosine,
  sd_rater_cosine,
  sd_human_pair_cosine,
  rho_row
) {
  expand.grid(
    beta_intercept_agreement = beta_intercept_agreement,
    regcheckhuman_agreement = regcheckhuman_agreement,
    n_papers = n_papers,
    n_dims = n_dims,
    number_of_raters = number_of_raters,
    raters_per_paper = raters_per_paper,
    beta_intercept_cosine = beta_intercept_cosine,
    regcheckhuman_cosine = regcheckhuman_cosine,
    sd_dimension_agreement = sd_dimension_agreement,
    sd_paper_agreement = sd_paper_agreement,
    sd_rater_agreement = sd_rater_agreement,
    sd_human_pair_agreement = sd_human_pair_agreement,
    sd_dimension_cosine = sd_dimension_cosine,
    sd_paper_cosine = sd_paper_cosine,
    sd_rater_cosine = sd_rater_cosine,
    sd_human_pair_cosine = sd_human_pair_cosine,
    rho_row = rho_row,
    KEEP.OUT.ATTRS = FALSE,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
}


run_simulations <- function(
  param_grid,
  n_reps = 200,
  seed = 1,
  # keep nuisance fixed (change if you want)
  sd_row_agreement = 0.7,
  sd_row_cosine = 0.09,
  clip_cosine_01 = TRUE,
  conf = 0.95,
  n_draws_delta = 4000
) {
  
  # set seed
  set.seed(seed)

  # assign scenario ids
  param_grid$scenario_id <- seq_len(nrow(param_grid))

  required_cols <- c(
    "beta_intercept_agreement", "regcheckhuman_agreement", "n_papers",
    "n_dims", "number_of_raters", "raters_per_paper",
    "beta_intercept_cosine", "regcheckhuman_cosine",
    "sd_dimension_agreement", "sd_paper_agreement", "sd_rater_agreement", "sd_human_pair_agreement",
    "sd_dimension_cosine", "sd_paper_cosine", "sd_rater_cosine", "sd_human_pair_cosine",
    "rho_row"
  )
  stopifnot(all(required_cols %in% names(param_grid)))

  # create object for storing output
  per_rep <- vector("list", nrow(param_grid) * n_reps)
  k <- 0

  # iterate scenarios
  for (s in seq_len(nrow(param_grid))) {
    parameter_grid <- param_grid[s, ]

    # run replications within each scenario
    for (r in seq_len(n_reps)) {
      k <- k + 1L

      # simulate dataset
      data <- simulate_agreement_and_cosine_data_shared_RE(
        n_papers = parameter_grid$n_papers,
        n_dims = parameter_grid$n_dims,
        beta_intercept_agreement  = parameter_grid$beta_intercept_agreement,
        regcheckhuman_agreement = parameter_grid$regcheckhuman_agreement,
        beta_intercept_cosine    = parameter_grid$beta_intercept_cosine,
        regcheckhuman_cosine   = parameter_grid$regcheckhuman_cosine,
        sd_dimension_agreement = parameter_grid$sd_dimension_agreement,
        sd_paper_agreement     = parameter_grid$sd_paper_agreement,
        sd_rater_agreement = parameter_grid$sd_rater_agreement,
        sd_human_pair_agreement = parameter_grid$sd_human_pair_agreement,
        sd_dimension_cosine   = parameter_grid$sd_dimension_cosine,
        sd_paper_cosine       = parameter_grid$sd_paper_cosine,
        sd_rater_cosine = parameter_grid$sd_rater_cosine,
        sd_human_pair_cosine = parameter_grid$sd_human_pair_cosine,
        rho_row = parameter_grid$rho_row,
        sd_row_agreement = sd_row_agreement,
        sd_row_cosine   = sd_row_cosine,
        number_of_raters = parameter_grid$number_of_raters,
        raters_per_paper = parameter_grid$raters_per_paper,
        seed = NULL,
        clip_cosine_01 = clip_cosine_01
      )

      # run analysis 
      ans <- analyze_simulated_data(data, conf = conf, n_draws_delta = n_draws_delta)

      # store only core replicate-level non-inferiority outputs
      per_rep[[k]] <- dplyr::bind_cols(
        tibble::tibble(scenario_id = parameter_grid$scenario_id, rep = r),
        tibble::as_tibble(ans) |>
          dplyr::select(
            ok_rq1, ok_rq2,
            rq1_margin, rq1_delta_hat, rq1_ci_lo, rq1_ci_hi, rq1_claim,
            rq2_margin, rq2_delta_hat, rq2_ci_lo, rq2_ci_hi, rq2_claim
          )
)
    }

    message(sprintf("Done scenario %d / %d", s, nrow(param_grid)))
  }

  # create raw simulation output of all reps and scenarios
  per_rep <- dplyr::bind_rows(per_rep)

  # split by scenario/param combo
  by_scenario <- split(per_rep, per_rep$scenario_id)

  # for each parameter combo, get statistics
  per_scenario <- do.call(rbind, lapply(by_scenario, function(data) {
    data.frame(
      scenario_id = data$scenario_id[1],
      n_reps = nrow(data),

      rq1_fit_rate = mean(data$ok_rq1),
      rq1_power_noninferior = mean(data$rq1_claim == "noninferior"),
      rq1_power_inferior = mean(data$rq1_claim == "inferior"),
      rq1_rate_inconclusive = mean(data$rq1_claim == "inconclusive"),
      rq1_delta_hat_mean = mean(data$rq1_delta_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
      rq1_noninferiority_margin_mean = mean(data$rq1_margin, na.rm = TRUE),

      rq2_fit_rate = mean(data$ok_rq2),
      rq2_power_noninferior = mean(data$rq2_claim == "noninferior"),
      rq2_power_inferior = mean(data$rq2_claim == "inferior"),
      rq2_rate_inconclusive = mean(data$rq2_claim == "inconclusive"),
      rq2_delta_hat_mean = mean(data$rq2_delta_hat, na.rm = TRUE),
      rq2_noninferiority_margin_mean = mean(data$rq2_margin, na.rm = TRUE),

      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
  }))

  # attach parameter columns once (unique per scenario)
  per_scenario <- merge(
    unique(param_grid),
    per_scenario,
    by = "scenario_id",
    all.x = TRUE
  )
  per_scenario <- per_scenario[order(per_scenario$scenario_id), ]

  list(
    power_by_scenario = per_scenario,
    per_rep_claims = per_rep
  )
}



```

# Step 4: Make parameter grid and run simulation

```{r}

# Choose values to run (edit as needed)
cosine_profiles <- tibble::tribble(
  ~cosine_profile, ~beta_intercept_cosine, ~regcheckhuman_cosine, ~sd_dimension_cosine, ~sd_paper_cosine,
  "optimistic",     0.84,                  0.80,                  0.05,                 0.05,
  "baseline",       0.80,                  0.74,                  0.06,                 0.06,
  "conservative",   0.74,                  0.66,                  0.08,                 0.08
)

param_grid <- dplyr::bind_rows(lapply(seq_len(nrow(cosine_profiles)), function(i) {
  profile <- cosine_profiles[i, ]

  make_param_grid(
    beta_intercept_agreement  = qlogis(c(0.75, 0.78)),
    regcheckhuman_agreement = qlogis(c(0.75, 0.78)),
    n_papers     = 100, # c(50, 100, 150),
    n_dims = 9,
    number_of_raters = 5,
    raters_per_paper = 2,
    beta_intercept_cosine    = profile$beta_intercept_cosine,
    regcheckhuman_cosine   = profile$regcheckhuman_cosine,
    sd_dimension_agreement = c(1),
    sd_paper_agreement     = c(1),
    sd_rater_agreement = c(0.2),
    sd_human_pair_agreement = c(0.2),
    sd_dimension_cosine   = profile$sd_dimension_cosine,
    sd_paper_cosine       = profile$sd_paper_cosine,
    sd_rater_cosine = c(0.04),
    sd_human_pair_cosine = c(0.04),
    rho_row            = c(0.3, 0.5)
  ) |>
    dplyr::mutate(cosine_profile = profile$cosine_profile)
}))

# Run the full simulation set
all_simulations <- run_simulations(
  param_grid = param_grid,
  n_reps = 10,      # increase for final planning
  seed = 123
)

# Results:
power_by_scenario <- all_simulations$power_by_scenario
per_rep_claims <- all_simulations$per_rep_claims

head(power_by_scenario)


```
